companies.name: Hindustan Aeronautics
companies.description: India's primary aircraft manufacturer. Produces Tejas fighter, Dhruv helicopter, Su-30 MKI overhaul. Loss would ground the IAF within months.

companies.name: Bharat Electronics
companies.description: Manufactures radar systems, electronic warfare suites, and battlefield communication for all three forces.

threats.method: Chip-level hardware backdoors in imported ICs

companies.name: Bharat Dynamics
companies.description: India's sole missile production agency. Produces Akash, NAG, MRSAM. Loss disarms ground-based air defenses.
protections: Hardware security modules for guidance code

companies.name: Mazagon Dock
companies.description: Builds Scorpene submarines and P-15B stealth destroyers. Controls India's undersea warfare capability.

companies.name: Garden Reach Shipbuilders
companies.description: Builds corvettes and patrol vessels for the Indian Navy. Key to littoral warfare capability.

companies.name: Cochin Shipyard
companies.description: Built INS Vikrant, India's first indigenous carrier. Loss halts carrier program for a generation.

companies.name: Larsen & Toubro
companies.description: Builds nuclear-sub pressure hulls, artillery, and critical civil/military infrastructure. Most diversified defense firm.

threats.method: SCADA attack on Smart City projects

companies.name: Bharat Forge
companies.description: Manufactures 155mm howitzers and critical forgings for aerospace, automotive, and defense.

companies.name: Mishra Dhatu Nigam
companies.description: Only Indian producer of maraging steel, superalloys, titanium alloys used in missiles, submarines, and aircraft.

threats.method: Rare-earth supply cutoff affecting alloy feedstock

companies.name: Astral Ltd
companies.description: Supplies industrial piping for defense facilities, naval bases, and critical infrastructure.

companies.name: Ashok Leyland
companies.description: Primary supplier of military trucks (LSHS, STALLION) to the Indian Army. Controls battlefield logistics mobility.

threats.method: ECU firmware attack on military fleets

companies.name: Mahindra & Mahindra
companies.description: Manufactures Armored Scorpio, Mine Protected Vehicles (MPV), and light armored vehicles for the Army and Para-SF.

companies.name: Solar Industries
companies.description: India's largest explosives company. Supplies detonators, warheads, and bulk explosives. Loss degrades Army and mining operations.

companies.name: Data Patterns
companies.description: Makes embedded systems, radar signal processors, and avionics for HAL and BEL. Small but single-source for critical subsystems.

companies.name: Reliance Industries
companies.description: World's largest refinery at Jamnagar. Fuels 40% of India's aviation and military fuel. Loss cascades across the entire economy.

threats.method: Undersea cable cut + Jamnagar precision strike

companies.name: ONGC
companies.description: India's largest oil producer. Offshore platforms in Mumbai High, KG Basin. Loss forces dependency on hostile exporters.

threats.method: Underwater drone attack on Mumbai High platforms

protections: Redundant SCADA with physical kill switches

companies.name: Indian Oil Corp
companies.description: Operates 11 refineries and 50,000-km pipeline network. Distribution backbone for civilian and military fuel.
protections: Rapid response teams at critical nodes
Isolated OT network with manual override

companies.name: Bharat Petroleum
companies.description: Manages strategic petroleum reserves and retail fuel. Critical buffer against supply shock.
protections: Physical reserve security with Army cover

companies.name: GAIL India
companies.description: Operates 15,000km of gas pipeline. Supplies gas to power plants and industry nationwide.

companies.name: Oil India
companies.description: Operates fields in Assam and Arunachal. Strategically vital for northeastern border supply — if cut, Army in the east has no local fuel.
protections: Army Quick Reaction Teams at Duliajan

companies.name: Petronet LNG
companies.description: Dahej and Kochi LNG terminals supply gas when domestic supply falls short. External dependency vulnerability.

companies.name: NTPC
companies.description: Generates 70GW of India's power. Attacks here turn off factories, hospitals, and military bases simultaneously.

threats.method: Precision missile strike on coal plants

protections: Missile defense coverage of major plants

companies.name: Power Grid Corp
companies.description: Operates 180,000km high-voltage transmission lines. The nerve that carries power from plants to cities.

threats.method: Coordinated drone strike on transformer hubs

companies.name: Coal India
companies.description: Supplies 80% of coal to thermal power plants. A strike or disruption here throttles India's entire electricity supply.

threats.impact: Power plants go dark in 10 days

companies.name: Tata Steel
companies.description: Only Indian maker of armor-grade steel for tanks and ships. Loss forces import of strategic material.

threats.method: Dumping cheap steel to close Indian mills

companies.name: JSW Steel
companies.description: Key steel supplier for bridges, rail, and construction. Hits here slow border road and bunker construction.

companies.name: Hindalco
companies.description: Produces aerospace-grade aluminum used in aircraft frames and missile bodies. Critical aerospace material.

companies.name: State Bank of India
companies.description: Largest public bank. Finances defense procurement, pays Army salaries. A run on SBI triggers state-level panic.

companies.name: HDFC Bank
companies.description: Largest private bank. Corporate credit backbone. Failure freezes private sector payroll and supply-chains.

threats.method: SWIFT system compromise for fraudulent transfers
Coordinated loan-book attack via shell companies

companies.name: ICICI Bank
companies.description: Finances India's export-import trade. Disruption stops defense imports and trade settlements.

companies.name: Axis Bank
companies.description: Third-largest private bank. Defense SME supply-chain financing. Stress here ripples into defense vendor payments.

threats.method: KYC data theft for mass impersonation

companies.name: Tata Consultancy Services
companies.description: Runs IT for IRCTC, Income Tax, defense payroll, banking backends. Single point of failure for government digital services.

companies.name: Infosys
companies.description: Finacle banking platform powers 100+ banks. A compromise here simultaneously destabilizes India's entire banking sector.

threats.impact: All banks on Finacle simultaneously vulnerable

companies.name: HCL Technologies
companies.description: Provides cybersecurity services for defense PSUs, manages critical government data centers.

threats.method: Compromise HCL to pivot to defense client networks
Defense project employee poaching by foreign actors

protections: Security clearance requirements for defense staff

companies.name: CDSL
companies.description: Central depository holding ownership records of 90M+ investors. Corruption or loss would destroy ownership evidence for all listed assets.

companies.name: Paytm (One97)
companies.description: UPI payments platform for 300M consumers. Disruption halts daily commerce and cash flow for small businesses.

companies.name: Bharti Airtel
companies.description: Provides 5G, undersea cables, and satellite communications. Army forward units use Airtel satellite links. Loss blacks out border comms.

threats.method: Undersea cable cut (Bay of Bengal chokepoints)
threats.impact: International internet + military data links severed

threats.method: BGP hijacking of Airtel routing tables

protections: Sub-sea cable redundancy via multiple routes

companies.name: Jio Financial Services
companies.description: New-age fintech built on Jio's 500M user base. Rapid growth creates new systemic risk vectors.

threats.method: Deepfake-based fraud at scale via Jio platform

protections: Phased feature rollout with red-team testing

companies.name: Adani Ports
companies.description: Controls Mundra (India's largest port), Hazira, and 12 other ports. 30% of India's cargo. Loss means defense imports and oil cannot land.

companies.name: Container Corp of India
companies.description: Moves containers between ports and inland depots via Indian Railways. Critical link for defense supply chains.
protections: Military escort for strategic cargo trains

companies.name: Rail Vikas Nigam
companies.description: Builds strategic rail to border areas (Ladakh, Arunachal). Loss stalls troop and supply movement to forward areas.

threats.method: Funding insurgent attacks on border rail construction
Bridge bombing on strategic rail line

companies.name: Ircon International
companies.description: Builds roads, rail, and tunnels in border areas (Himalayas, Northeast). Directly enables Army forward deployment.

threats.method: Bribing local contractors to delay projects
Attack on tunnel construction (Zoji La, etc.)

companies.name: Tata Motors
companies.description: Manufactures LPTA trucks for Army, ambulances, and civilian transport fleet that doubles as military logistics in wartime.

companies.name: UltraTech Cement
companies.description: Supplies cement for Army bunkers, border tunnels, and air-raid shelters. Backbone of military construction.

companies.name: Adani Enterprises
companies.description: Spans airports, roads, data centers, and defense. Single adversary action against Adani Group can destabilize multiple critical sectors.

threats.method: Targeting Adani-run airports as soft targets

companies.name: Siemens India
companies.description: Supplies switchgear, rail electrification, and industrial control systems. Embedded in power grid and rail infrastructure.

threats.method: Remote access to Siemens maintenance portals

companies.name: ABB India
companies.description: Automates power substations and industrial robots. Embedded in grid infrastructure — a backdoor here gives grid control.

companies.name: IndiGo (InterGlobe Aviation)
companies.description: Largest airline with 300+ aircraft. Commandeered under National Security Act for rapid troop/materiel airlifting.
protections: Fleet dispersal to secondary airfields in crisis

companies.name: DLF
companies.description: Major real estate / infrastructure developer. Builds cantonments, officer quarters, and urban housing for defense families.

threats.method: BIM software attack on defense construction blueprints

protections: Airgapped design systems for defense projects

companies.name: Sun Pharma
companies.description: Largest Indian pharma company. Supplies antibiotics, antivirals, and vaccines to Army medical. Loss compromises soldier healthcare in conflict.
